task: Write a Python function trimmed_mean(nums) returning the mean of the list after dropping exactly one occurrence of the minimum and one occurrence of the maximum, as a float. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The solution handles the edge case of an empty list correctly, but could be more precise in its implementation.
trace: To create the `trimmed_mean()` function, I first defined a conditional statement to handle an empty list, returning `None`. Then, I sorted the input list and calculated the median by accessing the middle element (rounded down using integer division). After that, I calculated the sum of the elements in the list excluding the minimum and maximum values and divided it by the number of remaining elements (n - 2) to obtain the trimmed mean as a float. The edge case I addressed is when the input list contains only one element, in which case the function returns that single value as the trimmed mean. By using concise and readable code, the function efficiently finds and returns the desired result while considering the given criteria.
